The total response time combines the individual database functions based on query distribution line.  
MySQL handles alpha times Q queries while Oracle takes beta times Q queries line.  
Sybase processes the remaining queries which equal one minus alpha minus beta times Q line.  
Substitute these values into each specific time function to form the total expression line.  
The resulting equation represents the sum of all three database response times line.  
Optimization requires finding alpha and beta values that minimize this total function line.  
Given constants define the specific behavior of each database under load line.  
With Q equal to 1000 we can calculate the numerical value for each term line.  
Solving the partial derivatives with respect to alpha and beta yields the optimum line.  
The solution process involves setting the gradient of the total function to zero line.  
Numerical methods are often needed because the equations are non linear and complex line.  
The calculated proportions will balance the load to achieve the fastest response line.  
This approach ensures that no single database becomes a bottleneck for the system line.  
The original model provides the foundation for this performance optimization strategy line.  
Final values for alpha and beta depend on the specific constants provided earlier line.
